name: sketch-workflow
description: Sketch is the FLAC 9.0 name for workflows formerly called extrusion. A sketch is a 2D design made from points, edges, blocks, and mesh controls. FLAC2D can generate zones directly; FLAC3D extrudes the sketch before creating zones.

objects.role: Named sketch collection; commands operate on the active set.
Coordinate anchors for edges and blocks.
Line or curved boundaries with zoning controls.
Closed shape meshed as regular structured or irregular unstructured mesh.
Mesh sizing, optimization, and meshing control parameters.
Pre-defined section primitives (circle / polygon / rectangle) to seed a sketch.

notes: Use 'sketch mesh target-size <float>' / 'sketch mesh reset' / 'sketch mesh mode <mixed|quad>' / 'sketch mesh type <mixed|unstructured>' for mesh control.

Use 'sketch set automatic-zone <size|total|direction|edge> <value>' to drive automatic zoning. Sub-keyword types: size <int>=1, total <int>=1, direction <both|construction|extrusion>, edge <float 1e-5..1e5>.
'sketch mesh automatic' is NOT a valid subcommand.
FLAC2D generates zones from the 2D sketch.
FLAC3D requires extrusion/section setup before 3D zones are generated.

rejected_subcommands_note: 'sketch segment' and 'sketch path' are NOT valid FLAC2D 9.0 subcommands ('Bad conversion of parameter number 2'). Only block / edge / mesh / point / section / set are accepted after 'sketch'.
version_notes: In FLAC 9.0, sketch replaces the older extrude naming.
Some legacy documentation and command resources may still use extruder/extrude terminology.
